question.text: Trading in commodity derivatives can only be conducted between:

answer.text: Members of an approved exchange that has obtained a certificate of registration from SEBI
Any two parties who agree to trade

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Futures trading can only be conducted between members of SEBI-approved exchanges. Exchange membership and SEBI recognition are prerequisites. **The Trap:** Not bilateral (that's OTC forwards). Not restricted to banks or government. Exchange membership is mandatory. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers exchange-traded derivatives requirements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always verify that your broker is a registered member of the relevant commodity exchange — unregistered entities offering commodity trading are illegal.
